mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption forecasts, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and increases in consumer prices indicate stagflationary margin compression; this interaction generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances when RSAFS declines month-over-month while CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month rolling average.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production accelerations predict positive S&P 500 returns only during monetary easing cycles when financing costs are falling; during tightening, supply gains are offset by higher discount rates.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month increases fail to predict positive 60d returns with Rank IC > 0.04 during FEDFUNDS < 2.5% regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals contraction in construction activity and downstream durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 positive 5d returns in > 50% of months when HOUST < 1200.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 5.0% creates a restrictive monetary environment that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced credit availability, producing negative forward weekly returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered weeks when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in payroll employment and consumer prices trigger fears of a wage-price spiral and subsequent aggressive Fed tightening that outweigh demand benefits, producing negative S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL fail to produce negative 20d returns with Rank IC > 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales tracks nominal consumer spending and corporate revenue growth; sustained increases predict positive S&P 500 forward monthly returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month increases do not predict positive 20d returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
